questions.question: Explain the difference between combinational and sequential circuits with examples of each.
keyPoints: Combinational: output depends only on current inputs
Sequential: output depends on current inputs AND past inputs (memory)
Examples: Adders, MUX, Decoder (combinational); Flip-flops, Counters, Registers (sequential)
No feedback in combinational; feedback present in sequential

questions.question: Design a 4-to-1 multiplexer. Write its truth table and draw the logic diagram.
keyPoints: 4 data inputs (I0,I1,I2,I3)
2 select lines (S0,S1)
1 output (Y)
Select lines choose which input passes to output
Truth table showing all 16 combinations
Implementation using AND-OR or tristate buffers

questions.question: What is a flip-flop? Explain the working of a D flip-flop with its truth table and characteristic equation.
keyPoints: Flip-flop: bistable memory element storing 1 bit
D flip-flop: simplest, transfers D to Q on clock edge
Has set and reset capability (may be asynchronous)
Characteristic equation: Q(next) = D
Edge-triggered (usually positive edge)

questions.question: Differentiate between synchronous and asynchronous counters.
keyPoints: Synchronous: all flip-flops clocked by same signal
Asynchronous (ripple): each flip-flop clocks next
Synchronous: faster, no cumulative delay
Asynchronous: simpler, but propagation delay adds up
Ripple effect causes glitches in async counters

questions.question: Explain the Mealy and Moore state machines with a suitable example.
keyPoints: Mealy: output depends on current state AND current input
Moore: output depends only on current state
Mealy has fewer states typically
Moore output delay by one clock cycle
Example: Sequence detector (Mealy vs Moore implementation)

questions.question: What is a decoder? Design a 2-to-4 decoder with enable input and explain its working.
keyPoints: Decoder: n inputs to 2^n outputs
2-to-4 decoder: 2 inputs (A,B), 4 outputs (D0-D3)
Enable input for activation
Only one output HIGH at a time (or LOW)
Used in memory addressing, 7-segment display

questions.question: Define setup time and hold time. Why are they important in sequential circuits?
keyPoints: Setup time: input must be stable before clock edge
Hold time: input must be stable after clock edge
Violations cause metastability
Metastability: undefined output between 0 and 1
Critical for reliable flip-flop operation
Determines maximum clock frequency

questions.question: Design a mod-10 (decade) synchronous counter using JK flip-flops. Write the excitation table, state table, and draw the logic diagram.
keyPoints: Mod-10 counts 0 to 9 (10 states)
Requires 4 flip-flops (BCD)
State encoding: 0000 to 1001
Excitation table for JK flip-flops
K-map simplification for J and K inputs
Logic diagram with clock to all FFs
Self-correcting for invalid states (optional)

questions.question: Explain the full adder circuit. Design a 4-bit parallel adder using full adders and explain carry propagation.
keyPoints: Full adder: adds 3 bits (A,B,Cin)
Sum = A XOR B XOR Cin
Carry = (A AND B) OR (Cin AND (A XOR B))
4-bit parallel adder: cascaded 4 full adders
Ripple carry: slow due to sequential carry propagation
Carry look-ahead: faster, generates carries in parallel
Formulas: Carry generate and propagate

questions.question: Design a sequence detector that detects the pattern '101' using Mealy machine. Write state diagram, state table, and implementation using D flip-flops.
keyPoints: Mealy machine for '101' sequence
States: S0 (no input), S1 (1 received), S10 (10 received)
State diagram with output标注
State encoding (2 flip-flops needed)
Excitation table for D FFs
K-maps for D inputs
Logic implementation

questions.question: Explain the working of a shift register. Discuss different types of shift registers (SIPO, PISO, SISO, PIPO) with applications.
keyPoints: Shift register: stores and shifts data bits
Serial In Parallel Out (SIPO): converts serial to parallel
Parallel In Serial Out (PISO): converts parallel to serial
Serial In Serial Out (SISO): delay element
Parallel In Parallel Out (PIPO): parallel storage
Applications: data transfer, arithmetic operations, UART
